v0_to_v1_prompt_delta: v0 prompt run scored 1.000 on v0 (0.952 on v1 strict-item re-judge). v1 prompt run scores 1.000 on v1 — prompt update successfully pulled the analysis up to the calibration baseline, especially on trend-anti-claim which the v0 prompt didn't surface as required output.

item_scores.reasoning: 5 alternatives named: Azure-OpenAI, Vertex AI, Brazilian SI providers, status quo (multi-vendor pilots — non-obvious 'do nothing decisively' alternative), Bedrock-mediated Claude (non-obvious internal alternative). Both non-obvious alternatives explicitly included.

4 attributes, each explicitly labeled by structural type (Identity, Contractual, Distribution, Architectural) and contrasted against named alternatives. Each demonstrably absent from named alternatives.

All 4 themes connect attributes to specific outcomes with magnitudes: 'weeks-to-months off enterprise procurement cycles,' 'auditable LGPD/ANPD compliance narrative,' 'avoids forced re-contracting,' 'reduced model-supply-chain risk and direct escalation path.'

Chief Risk Officers and Heads of AI Governance at Brazilian Tier-1 banks (Itaú, Bradesco, Banco do Brasil, Nubank, BTG-tier), 5,000+ employees, São Paulo HQ, with named buying-condition (multi-vendor pilots running, LGPD gating production, wants model-maker-direct relationship). Plus secondary best-fit specified.

Default category named ('Enterprise generative AI platform' — Azure-OpenAI and Vertex AI's territory) with why-it-loses argument (distribution, brand, price). Chosen category 'Frontier AI from the safety lab — direct from the model maker, LGPD-ready' with explicit gains (differentiation on identity + contract structure unfakeable by hyperscalers, alignment with LGPD gate) AND explicit give-ups (broad mid-market, horizontal play, price-per-token, Bedrock-only shops, speed-to-pilot).

Specific concrete rejection: 'Heads of IT at Brazilian federal-government agencies and SOEs (Petrobras, Banco do Brasil public-sector, federal ministries) operating under Lei 14.133/2021 procurement rules that favor locally headquartered providers and require on-prem or sovereign-cloud.' Role + named industry/segment + 3 specific blocking criteria (no Brazilian entity, public procurement scoring penalty, no on-prem).

Single trend invoked (LGPD enforcement + ANPD AI guidance) with specific rationale tying to identity + architectural attributes. Plus 3 trends explicitly rejected with rationale: 'AI transformation in LATAM' (trend slop), 'Sovereign AI / digital sovereignty' (works against, no sovereign-cloud story), 'Portuguese-language LLM leadership' (overpromises vs. local incumbents).

'For Chief Risk Officers and AI governance leaders at Brazilian Tier-1 banks and regulated fintechs running multi-vendor LLM pilots under tightening LGPD enforcement, Anthropic is the frontier AI offer direct from the safety lab — giving you a contractual relationship with the model maker and an auditable safety framework that hyperscaler-bundled alternatives like Azure-OpenAI and Vertex AI cannot structurally provide.' All 3 ingredients. The 'cannot structurally provide' clause excludes Microsoft and Google by structure.

v1 STRICT EXPLICITLY ENGAGED. Each of 4 attributes labeled by structural type. Identity: 'safety research as core company thesis, not compliance add-on.' Contractual: 'direct counterparty relationship with the lab that trains the frontier model.' Distribution: 'dual-channel reach — Anthropic-direct AND embedded in AWS Bedrock.' Architectural: 'model behavior shaped by published, externally legible safety framework (RSP + Constitutional AI).' No 'better/faster' claims; all four are structural.

v1 STRICT EXPLICITLY ENGAGED. Named role (Heads of IT) + named industry segment (federal-government agencies, SOEs, with named examples Petrobras, Banco do Brasil public-sector, federal ministries) + 3 specific blocking criteria (no Brazilian legal entity, Lei 14.133/2021 scoring penalty for foreign-headquartered vendors, on-prem requirement rules out API-only delivery). Plus secondary worst-fit (BRL-price-sensitive SMBs/devs) with rationale.

v1 STRICT EXPLICITLY ENGAGED. Dedicated 'Trends DELIBERATELY NOT invoked, and why' subsection. Three specific trends rejected with case-specific rationale: 'AI transformation in LATAM' (trend slop, dilutes LGPD claim), 'Sovereign AI / digital sovereignty' (works against — no sovereign-cloud story), 'Portuguese-language LLM leadership' (no defensible claim vs. local incumbents). Each rejection tied to a specific structural reason.
